name: The White House
subject: President Trump visits the wall
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac492a1fab60cb39d98c3ef72370b5565e8138d69bcbbbfc6796ab6637275ecef8f7c4ca9bd4efff1d616f5ac1b00d60a16

President Trump visits the wall

Today, President Donald J. Trump travels from Washington to Southern California, where he will examine eight prototypes for the proposed border wall with Mexico. The samples range in height from 18 to 30 feet.

“While Congress considers funding for fiscal year 2018, other wall program activities are moving forward,” Paul Koscak of U.S. Customs and Border Protection writes. Those activities include planning and design, as well as upgrades along less fortified or outdated parts of the southwest barrier.

The President’s visit today comes as more and more Americans express support for his four-pronged approach to immigration reform. In addition to the majorities that support tougher border security and a permanent resolution for DACA, more than 8 in 10 voters want a system that selects individuals based on their education and skills—not one that chooses immigrants based on whether they have relatives already in the United States.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac4b263e0d2af25d8d88e36492682295fe1182c19c6ee52079a01a586cc9be412e52468e3138e950fc9b8686c81a5550b1a
Watch President Trump’s weekly address on immigration and sanctuary cities.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac4bc6289f8fe35c3082fceb45699e55db7725ece85c2342044ad9db227f5373183f878049e543168735fd077c35ce2baa6
New poll shows overwhelming support for President Trump’s immigration priorities.

You should be in charge of your healthcare information

“Interoperability” isn’t a word most Americans hear every day. But when it comes to the future of healthcare in the United States, few technological developments may prove to be more important.

In healthcare, interoperability means being able to share patient data in an instant—regardless of which hospital, pharmacy, or laboratory has the information—and being able to do so with complete reliability and privacy protection. Last week, White House Senior Adviser Jared Kushner laid out the Trump Administration’s plans in this area at the HIMSS18 conference in Las Vegas, Nevada.

“I could not understand why medical records from the Department of Defense could not be seamlessly transferred to the Veterans' Administration,” Kushner said. “There is overwhelming consensus: America needs better access to patient data and interoperability now.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac455e65dbb7fe9ac0c4486f50019cee25980bf69a4200c8d4799799fd8e7480b659f90cbb9b3e707dee8da72e472c7be20
Read more about the Trump Administration’s plan to put you in charge of your health information.

‘You were Houston strong’

In the same year that Houston battled the challenges of rebuilding from a destructive hurricane, the Houston Astros earned the city’s first baseball World Series Championship. The Astros visited the White House yesterday, where President Trump congratulated the team on a hard-fought series win.

“It’s a really befitting tribute—what was really a show of world spirit and Houston Strong,” President Trump said. “You were Houston Strong.”

The President explained that the Astros were true champions both on and off the field. “When [Hurricane] Maria ravaged Puerto Rico weeks later, the Houston Astros redoubled their assistance, and many of them went there and helped,” he said.

“It really exemplifies what greatness is all about.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac459ffebb82a8c20444be52ae372b416da14e303559387fcb8d96c67a9aa5a097b008ed74894130667cc87a8172749855f
See photos from President Trump’s visit with the Astros yesterday.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and the 2017 World Series Champion Houston Astros | March 12, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump travels to San Diego, California, to review Border Wall Prototypes. The President will then deliver remarks to members of the military.

This morning, Vice President Mike Pence will participate in a Senate Republican Policy Lunch. Later, the Vice President will participate in a series of meetings with lawmakers.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac4c770cc778312b7272fff363b70097976fb2c3d1cb42a7746baa03702ad0a238058d2ed5541460cd3c4a885067163fa7d
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=395214aea9753ac414f4d884bc6238540c7b259499a3781a8071b99137d66a16ebb8394df5285a622509698c75ff87356418064f6cbb3e06
Contact the White House |

name: Bob =?UTF-8?Q?Fertik=2C=20Democrats=2Ecom?=
subject: Jeff Sessions
body:
Dear Activist,

Last week we learned Donald Trump and Jeff Sessions are filing a lawsuit against my home state of California for refusing to comply with their anti-immigrant agenda.

This is a shameful stunt designed to intimidate California officials and endanger our public safety. It won’t work. We will always fight to uphold the core values of our nation. That means standing up for the idea that America is a nation of immigrants and our diversity makes us a stronger country.

No state in the union has more immigrants -- documented and undocumented -- than California, and they’re counting on us to fight for them.

That’s why I’m asking:

Can you sign my petition denouncing Trump and Sessions’ repeated attacks on California and our immigrant communities? Together, whether you live in California or not, we can send an unmistakable message that we won’t be intimidated. (https://secure.kamalaharris.org/page/s/acq_dems_180313_sessions?source=em180313-dems)

Sessions came to California personally to formally announce the lawsuit -- and I believe it is crucial we respond with a powerful show of grassroots solidarity.

This is a moment where we must measure up to the ideals of our nation and fight for what we believe in, even if it means supposed leaders like Trump and Sessions come after us.

Thanks for all you do,

Kamala Harris
U.S. Senator, California

SIGN THE PETITION (https://secure.kamalaharris.org/page/s/acq_dems_180313_sessions?source=em180313-dems)

name: Tom Wolf
subject: re: ethics reform
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom Wolf for GovernorContributions or gifts to Tom Wolf for Governor are not tax deductible
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Hi there,

Since the start of my campaign in 2014, I promised I would fight to change Harrisburg. And as soon as I took the oath of office to be your Governor, I've been working to make good on that promise.

On my first day in office, I ended pay-to-play contracting and banned members of my administration from taking gifts from lobbyists and special interests. I donate my salary to charity, and I refuse a state pension.

I've been working to curb the influence of special interests in Harrisburg, and rebuild Pennsylvanians' trust in government. Now I'm asking the legislature to do the same.

That's why yesterday, I announced my Citizens First Ethics Reform Plan.

This plan will make sure that our citizens are the ones calling the shots - not entrenched special interests.

**[Add your name and call on Harrisburg to pass the Citizens First Ethics Reform Plan->](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfe5/73a2bc12/3fd92f44/1fccfa31/2227392157/VEsH/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/pass-the-citizens-first-ethics-reform-plan?utm_campaign=em18_180313_ethics_engg_clq_act-pa__tw&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180313_ethics_engg_clq_act-pa__tw&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+and+call+on+Harrisburg+to+")**

Here's what my Ethics Reform Plan includes:
* **Gift Ban for Public Officials:** I banned members of my administration from accepting gifts, and I believe all public officials should be held to the same standard.
* **No Budget, No Pay:** The influence of special interests in Harrisburg has crippled our budget process. That's why I'm calling for a "No Budget, No Pay" bill to stop paying legislators and their top staff, as well as the governor and top officials in the executive branch, until a complete budget is passed.
* **Campaign Finance Reform:** Pennsylvanians need to have confidence that decisions made by their government are for the people, not the special interests with the deepest pockets. That's why I've proposed new campaign finance laws that would place limits on contributions to candidates, implement aggregate limits for races, place sensible restrictions on PACs, and strengthen reporting and disclosure requirements.
* **Implementing Pay-to-Play Protections:** I'm supporting broader "pay-to-play" provisions that require the disclosure of campaign contributions made by parties seeking contracts.
* **Outside Income Transparency:** I want to require public officials to disclose sources, the type of work, and amount of outside income received.
* **Require Receipts for Public Official Expenses:** In my administration, employees pay for expenses and provide receipts and then are reimbursed. But receipts are not required for all officials to be reimbursed with taxpayer dollars. That's why I'm proposing a system that would only allow reimbursements for a reasonable cost of travel, lodging, or food with a receipt.

Since I've taken office, we've made significant progress towards a more transparent government that works best for Pennsylvanians. And I know the Citizens First plan will keep us moving in the right direction.

**[So I'm asking, please add your name and send a message to Harrisburg to pass the Citizens First Ethics Reform Plan -> ](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfe5/73a2bc12/3fd92f44/1fccfa30/2227392157/VEsE/ "https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/s/pass-the-citizens-first-ethics-reform-plan?utm_campaign=em18_180313_ethics_engg_clq_act-pa__tw&utm_medium=email&utm_source=wolf&source=em18_180313_ethics_engg_clq_act-pa__tw&utm_content=2+-+So+Irsquom+asking+please+add+your+name+a")**

Thank you for all your support of this campaign. With you working alongside me, I know we'll continue to make real change in Harrisburg.

Thank you,

Tom Wolf

If that is not your preferred email address, you can update your information [here](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfe5/73a2bc12/3fd92f44/1fccfa37/2227392157/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://action.wolfforpa.com/changeaddress?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=tomwolf&utm_content=3+-+here&utm_campaign=em18_180305_lamb_fnd_gq_ful__tw&source=em18_180305_lamb_fnd_gq_ful__tw")

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: We are at a critical moment for the future of the Democratic Party, and for our country.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
The political revolution is on the move, not only in progressive states but in conservative areas as well.
#
Sisters and Brothers -
I want to tell you about a very exciting trip that I took last weekend to Texas and Arizona. That follows the trip we took several weeks ago to Illinois, Iowa, Wisconsin and Michigan. Of these six states, Trump won five. And, in Illinois, where Trump lost, progressives are taking on the old and moribund Democratic machine.
The very good news is: the political revolution is on the move, not only in progressive states but in conservative areas as well. You have heard me say it a million times. This country will not change if the Democratic Party continues to ignore half the states in this country, including some of the poorest.
We're not going to transform the politics of the United States by just doing fundraisers with wealthy people in New York and California or starting Super PACs funded by special interests. We have got to take the fight for economic, social, racial and environmental justice to every county in every state in this country.
And the good news is that is exactly what is beginning to happen. **This year, there are more candidates than ever on the ballot - in progressive and conservative areas - who support Medicare for all, free tuition at public colleges and universities, a $15 minimum wage, gun safety legislation and our entire progressive agenda.**
In many instances, these candidates are fighting against a political establishment that tells them they cannot win running on a progressive agenda, and they are fighting against a financial elite who will spend anything to stop them. **We should work as hard as we can to elect those candidates, and I intend to - just as I did this past weekend. But I cannot do it alone. So I have to ask:**
**[Can I count on you to make a $3 donation to support my work traveling the country and rallying people to support progressive causes and candidates who need our help? I would not ask if it were not so important. I cannot do it without your help. We're in this together.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")**
That's what the political revolution is about and that's what, with your support, we're doing. And, trust me, it's not me alone. Hundreds of volunteers helped on this trip and I spoke alongside of great progressives like Jim Hightower of Texas and Nina Turner, the national president of Our Revolution.
Do you want to hear about success? In Texas earlier last week, progressive candidates have either won or advanced to runoffs in 19 out of 27 races that were contested, and voter turnout shot way up statewide.
On our visit to Texas we had thousands of people come out in San Antonio and Lubbock. Yes. In Lubbock, one of the most conservative areas in Texas, where Trump won 2-1, we had a standing room only crowd of 1,300. This is a place where the Democratic Party is incredibly weak and where the local newspaper claimed no progressive rally like ours had taken place in a "long time, if ever."
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")
_(The massive crowd at the rally in San Antonio, Texas)_
In Lubbock, 46 percent of the population is Latino or African American and the overwhelming majority of the community is poor or working class. Yet like in many other rural parts of the country, the Democratic Party has conceded the area and right-wing Republicans keep winning. That's pathetic, and we're in the process of changing that.
And what was our message in Trump country? It was that the political pundits are wrong when they say there are "blue states" like Vermont, "red states" like Texas, and "purple states" like Arizona, and that we need to be "realistic" in our goals because there are big differences between them all.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")
_(Bernie takes a moment to thank some volunteers in Lubbock, Texas)_
**Well, I don't believe that for a second and neither should you. I believe that in every state in this country, working people are struggling. There must be - and there will be - a progressive government that fights for all of us, not just the 1 percent.**
Because no matter where you live, the vast majority of people don't believe in a Republican agenda that provides huge tax breaks to billionaires while cutting Social Security, Medicare and Medicaid.
No matter where you live, the vast majority of the people understand that in the wealthiest country in the history of the world people should not be forced to work two or three jobs to pay the bills because we have a national minimum wage of $7.25 an hour - a starvation wage. They also understand that women should not be earning 80 percent or less of what men make for doing the same work.
No matter where you live, we all get sick, we all have accidents and we all need health care. And we now see a growing majority in this country who understand that health care is a right, not a privilege. We also see more and more people who want a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system.
No matter where you live, the majority of people understand that is is insane and counterproductive to the best interests of our country that hundreds of thousands of bright people cannot afford to go to college and that millions of others leave school with a mountain of debt that burdens them for decades.
No matter where you live, more and more Americans, including gun owners, understand that we need to do everything possible to end the unspeakable level of gun violence that is currently taking place. They know that, no matter what the NRA thinks, we need to pass commonsense gun safety legislation which keeps guns out of the hands of people who should not have them.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")
_(A huge turnout for Bernie at the Orpheum Theater in Phoenix, Arizona)_
The final stop of our trip was Phoenix, Arizona. When I arrived on Saturday evening a young women working at the hotel I was staying at told me something that should shock and disgust you. In 2016, she waited in line for seven hours to vote in Arizona. Imagine that - seven hours in line to participate in our democracy. That she waited seven hours in line to vote for me was extremely humbling. But what about all those who couldn't wait, because of their families or their jobs? Every one of us should work to end voter suppression in Arizona and many other states across the country. We need a vigorous democracy in which every single person can participate in our electoral process.
At our rally the next day thousands of people came out to talk about the importance of comprehensive immigration reform that is based on justice, not racism; the urgent need to ensure health care is a right for every man, woman and child in this country; and the importance of energizing and electing progressives in Arizona and all across the country in 2018.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")
_(And the overflow crowd outside the event in Phoenix, Arizona)_
We can elect progressives in Arizona in 2018. We can elect them in Texas as well. We can elect them in New York and North Dakota, Mississippi, Kansas, South Carolina and Idaho.
And we must. Because the continued decline of the middle class, grotesque levels of income and wealth inequality, disastrous trade policies and an inadequate educational system affect all of us.
This year, I am going to campaign hard to elect progressives in communities Democrats have long written off. But I cannot do it alone. I need your help to make it happen:
**[Make a $3 donation to support my work traveling the country and rallying people to support progressive causes and candidates? I would not ask if it were not so important.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")**
We are at a critical moment for the future of the Democratic Party, and for our country. As progressives we are making progress, but much more needs to be done. Trump is trying to divide our country up through racism, bigotry and xenophobia. We are bringing people together in the fight for justice and dignity for all.
Thank you for your support in realizing that goal.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

****[DONATE](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-pva?refcode=em180313-t1-full")****
** **
** **

name: Jim Zogby, Our Revolution
subject: The DNC must change
body:
This is the year when the voters must decide, not the party insiders
#
Friends,
This is the year when the voters must decide, not the party insiders. Just this month progressive Texas Democratic Congressional candidate Laura Moser defeated infighting and attacks from her own party. She did so by appealing to voters with bold ideas and a vision for the future. This wasn't the first time that establishment insiders tried to put their thumb on the scale to defeat a progressive candidate. But we are committed to making it the last.
For the past year, as Sanders' appointees to the Unity Reform Commission, we have been working to strengthen the voice of our party's grassroots in all levels of primaries and caucuses. When we finished our work, all members of the Unity Reform Commission, the DNC Chairman Tom Perez, and Vice Chair Keith Ellison embraced our recommendations. Yet, this weekend, the DNC Rules and Bylaws Committee watered down our agreed-upon, much-needed reforms that would open our party to independent voters and level the playing field for progressive candidates. It looks like it is up to us to push this change forward.
[**Our Revolution and our 500+ local groups have been organizing to reduce the number of superdelegates, increase access to voting in primaries and caucuses, and call for DNC transparency and accountability. Please chip in $5 to support our organizing efforts today.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7aca3/bed43eb/407d1407/75fe2012/2495997761/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-dnc?refcode=em180313-full")
This should be the year when the Democratic Party finally comes out of the shadows of bureaucracy and procedures and joins the rest of us as we create a progressive future that works for everyone. We have to reclaim the soul of the Democratic Party and make it work for ordinary people, not for establishment politicians.
[**The best way to hold people in power accountable is by building a grassroots movement large enough to challenge them. Can you chip in $5 to help Our Revolution build this movement with our 500+ local groups across the country?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7aca3/bed43eb/407d1407/75fe2012/2495997761/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-dnc?refcode=em180313-full")
I am committed to changing this party with you and Our Revolution to best represent the diversity that makes our country strong. Thank you for sharing our commitment to that change.
In solidarity,
Jim Zogby
Board Member
Our Revolution

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: Join Me at The March For Our Lives
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
It's hard to believe that it's been nearly two decades since the first massive Washington, DC, march for common sense gun laws. The Million Mom March drew over 750,000 moms and advocates to Washington on Mother's Day, May 14, 2000. That was the same year I worked with a Maryland mom, Carole Price, who had lost her son in an accidental gun death, to pass Maryland's mandatory handgun trigger lock law -- the first in the country.

Despite all their recent talk about gun safety, the National Rifle Association opposed this gun safety bill and worked hard to defeat us. But the bill passed the Maryland legislature on April 11, 2000, and President Bill Clinton came to the signing ceremony in Annapolis. Six years earlier, Congress had passed the Federal Assault Weapons Ban. It was a hopeful time, when people felt that real progress might be achievable. But as we all know, that was not the case. The federal Assault Weapons Ban expired, and the NRA worked harder than ever to tighten its grip on lawmakers to ensure that nothing would threaten their profitable business.

Today, as the daily carnage mounts and school massacres like the tragedy in Parkland continue, young people, many of whom were born the same year as the Million Mom March, are stepping up to demand long overdue action from their lawmakers on this critical issue. On March 24, I will be joining the March For Our Lives alongside these students, Maryland families, advocates from Marylanders to Prevent Gun Violence, and others.

[I**hope you can join us for the March For Our Lives, and for a pre-March event at the Stewart Mott House, 122 Maryland Avenue NE on Capitol Hill (a 5-minute walk from Union Station), for refreshments and discussion about what we need to do to finally take action to reduce gun violence.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de9187a/70cdc2ba/460003d3/39039fb/1954384379/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/join-me-before-the-march-for-our-lives?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+I+hope+you+can+join+us+for+the+March+For&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_INVITE_FULL_180313&source=CVH_EM_INVITE_FULL_180313") [](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de9187a/70cdc2ba/460003d3/39039f8/1954384379/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/join-me-before-the-march-for-our-lives?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_INVITE_FULL_180313&source=CVH_EM_INVITE_FULL_180313") Thank you for staying engaged and standing up for what's right.

Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

| | After Trump's improbable win in 2016, I felt sick to my stomach. Just like millions of progressives across the country. But we didn't wallow. **We got to work.** We refocused our energy and priorities. **We started looking toward the November midterms, knowing that taking back Congress is our best hope for stopping Trump and his far-right allies in Congress.** We even won a victory in court to create new and fairer congressional districts in Pennsylvania, significantly increasing our opportunities to win more seats. **[Our grassroots movement continues right here: chip in $4 today.](http://action.stackforpa.com/page/m/5fc04e81/7da2b91d/5ced0f6d/5c66c58d/487915808/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/mikestack?refcode=2018-03-13-priorities2&amount=4&recurring=1")** **And our work is paying off. Already we 've had big victories in state and local elections from Alabama to Virginia to Wisconsin. ** Tonight, we could very well add Pennsylvania to the list of states powering the blue tide. Conor Lamb is on the verge of a stunning upset. **This hard work is our message: we will not tolerate Trump 's betrayal of working Americans.** We will not stand for the GOP's hate-filled agenda or their attempts to rig the system with partisan gerrymandering. We will not sign over our nation to Wall Street bankers, to racists, to people seeking to divide us. **[Stand with me. Let's keep up the blue wave. Let's just say it, team: Democratic women are the key to stopping the GOP's attempts at taking away access to reproductive health care.
**With a Republican-controlled Senate, the Trump administration could easily appoint Supreme Court justices who would overturn _Roe v. Wade_ without batting an eye.**
That's why we need to stand steadfast with the pro-choice Democratic women -- women like Senator Maggie Hassan -- who ALWAYS defend the right to make one's own health care decisions.
[**Please help pro-choice Democratic women protect reproductive rights and show far-right Republicans that you won 't tolerate their anti-choice agenda by donating $3 or however much you can right now.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf23/2a380d31/5f5d738/f1e50a/1844320924/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxOC1yZXByb2R1Y3RpdmUtcmlnaHRzLWZyIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxODAzMTNfUmVwcm9SaWdodHNGUjNfYWN0IiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2018-reproductive-rights-fr?refcode=e20180313_ReproRightsFR3_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")

Look, a conservative Supreme Court is the surest way to undo the decades of progress we've made for women.
Holding on to each and every Democratic woman Senator we have (and adding to the ranks) is our best shot at not just defending access to safe reproductive health care, but ensuring that women can be fully included in society.
We've come a long way but we still have a bumpy road ahead of us, and we could really use you in this fight. clean_body: "" "" Let's just say it, team: Democratic women are the key to stopping the GOP's attempts at taking away access to reproductive health care. That's why we need to stand steadfast with the pro-choice Democratic women -- women like Senator Maggie Hassan -- who ALWAYS defend the right to make one's own health care decisions. "" If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. "" "" "" "" Look, a conservative Supreme Court is the surest way to undo the decades of progress we've made for women. Holding on to each and every Democratic woman Senator we have (and adding to the ranks) is our best shot at not just defending access to safe reproductive health care, but ensuring that women can be fully included in society. We've come a long way but we still have a bumpy road ahead of us, and we could really use you in this fight. "" Thank you, Team Maggie ""
